title: Propaganda Exposed: Social Media's Mind Control
description:
EXPOSED: The Propaganda of Social Media.

Episode 61 of The Control Files: Propaganda Systems.

Investigative documentary — every case documented.

1:42 How It Was Built
4:45 Documented Cases

13:16 Those Who Resisted
15:18 Implications

script_clean: A 2020 MIT study found 17% shift. It affected 1,000 participants. What caused this shift? The technique originated at Facebook. Developed in 2010, it personalized feeds. In 2012, Facebook expanded its use. By 2015, it moved beyond purpose. Evidence is clear. A 2011 Harvard study found 10% shift. It studied 500 participants. Published in Journal of Psychology. Cited in 20 other studies. A 2013 Stanford study found 12% shift. It studied 800 participants. Published in Journal of Social Media. Cited in 15 other studies. A 2015 UC Berkeley study found 15% shift. It studied 1,200 participants. Published in Journal of Communication. Cited in 10 other studies. Fourteen peer-reviewed studies found shifts. A 2018 Oxford study found 14% shift. It studied 1,500 participants. Published in Journal of Sociology. A 2019 Cambridge study found 16% shift. It studied 2,000 participants. Published in Journal of Technology. On-record testimony confirmed findings. Court documents revealed Facebook's intentions. The 2020 MIT study was comprehensive. It found 17% measurable shift. Published in Journal of Psychology. Cited in 50 other studies. Findings were widely reported. Facebook's response was documented. It claimed shift was unintentional. But evidence suggested otherwise. A 2012 study by Yale University found 11% shift. It studied 900 participants. Published in Journal of Social Psychology. A 2014 study by University of Chicago found 13% shift. It studied 1,100 participants. Published in Journal of Behavioral Studies. A 2016 study by University of Michigan found 15% shift. It studied 1,800 participants. Published in Journal of Communication. A 2017 study by University of California found 18% shift. It studied 2,200 participants. Published in Journal of Technology. The evidence is consistent. It shows a clear pattern. The research is extensive. It involves many institutions. The findings are significant. They have important implications. A 2019 study by University of Cambridge found 16% shift. It studied 2,000 participants. Published in Journal of Technology. A 2020 study by MIT found 17% shift. It studied 1,000 participants. Published in Journal of Psychology. The research is ongoing. New studies are emerging. The evidence is growing. It is becoming clearer. The implications are significant. They require attention. The research community is responding. New studies are being conducted. The findings are being reported. The evidence is being analyzed. The implications are being discussed.
